sentences.text: Hey there, Lee.
Can you hear me?
All right.
Hey, Phil.
Yes, how are you doing?
I'm doing all right this afternoon.
How about yourself?
Good.
Thanks a million for uh taking some time to connect.

I uh I've been really keen to get in touch with you just because, you know, as, as I've come across the account, I wanted to point out a few things that I think were a little out of date, highlight some things that are coming into the platform, some things that I get that you guys have access to uh and then talk about what the plan was for uh for the next year.
And of course, an understanding of how you guys are using apartment IQ and, and what usage looks like because I think I saw that you were probably one of the big users.
Um But wanted to see if there's opportunity there at apartment IQ.
Of course, we're not charging by the head.
So I always like to encourage as many folks to get in and then I can run training and plan engagement from there if that's all right.
Yeah, absolutely
sweet.
So I guess just to kind of kick it off.
Tell me a little bit about how you're finding apartment IQ lately.
Um, there's been a few changes in there.
Talk to me about your experience so far.
Yeah.
Admittedly I probably have not been using it as much the past couple of months.
What we do, I should say what we used to do is set our market rents for an entire year at a time.
So, leading into January in a typical year, we would set an across the board market rate increase.
Um And pretty much just sit pat for a year at a time.
Then with the changing environment that we've, you know, bear witness to, we wanted to make that commitment quarterly.

So what I was doing pretty much every three months was to look at our market survey information that we put together internally, you know, go over the competition, see where we stack up and adjust those market rents every quarter.
And when I, I got talking initially with rentable folks and they laid out what this software really could be.
I thought it would be a good addition to the full analysis that we do on a quarterly basis to, to really set our market rents as we review them.
So II, I won't say that I review or rely entirely on it.
I use a couple of different sources and this is one of the, you know, ingredients to the equation as we set our market each quarter.
Um But I like the, the information.
It does provide the way that it packages.
It, um, it's definitely more accessible and at your fingertips than doing manual Google searches to pull information from our competitors.
So, no, it's been, uh I think a valuable piece to us.
Um, most recently, I think those market or executive analysis, summaries that have gone out are helpful.

Um I think that was really for me on this call, you know, one of the biggest goals was to walk through, um, one of those reports and just make sure that I'm packaging it to our leadership group the, the right way.
Sure.
Yeah.
And I think um there's gonna be some really exciting changes coming to that as well.
Uh I actually just this past Friday.
Uh So as recently as last week looked at products markup of the few changes that are coming in the short term.
Um and I'll kind of highlight what you'll expect to see.
Nothing crazy is changing.
Uh But hopefully the changes are going to make it easier for someone to take a look at this, um, at a glance and understand which direction markets are going.
Which direction is this number moving, right?
We'll have trend lines going up down arrows that let you know what does this number mean compared to last week?
Right?
Unless you're looking at that every week, it can be hard to know that.
So we'll definitely talk about that.
Um And then you mentioned of course, Apartment IQ fitting in, in that, you know, part of your, your sauce, it's an ingredient.
Can you talk to me a little bit about the other tools you use?
Just that I can kind of understand where apartment IQ fits and, and kind of pass that along to product team and so that they can understand how it complements a suite.
Yeah.
Um we put together our own really spreadsheet of all our competition.
We input the rents that we're able to pull or observe out of the marketplace.
Um We look at things like age of property and just really trying to get our true cops.

I'd say if anything that's been part of the struggle with department IQ is that there's either some locations or competition that we can't extract or scrape the information from and the remaining data set is maybe not of apples to apples comparison.
So, yeah, yeah.

So there's some manual tweaking any time that I'm really like if I'm trying to drill down into Liberty Square as opposed to a particular property, you know, I've got to do some manually manual editing in the apartment IQ to make sure that our rent cops are a true one for one.
Whereas with our internal one, you know, we've catered that to be an exact match as we would wanna compare properties for.
Got it.
That makes sense.
That makes sense.
I'm wondering when you have this spreadsheet.
Is that in Power B I or some, or, or one of those business intelligence warehouses or are we working in Excel?
Yeah.
Google Sheets.
It's, uh, something, we have a couple of property accountants that can pull that information together, uh, pretty easily and quickly.
Um, they know what properties to be going through and pulling information from.
We have our, you know, some locations that don't put their rates up on, uh, you know, apartment dot com or don't integrate with yardi and therefore are not getting scraped.
We'll have our rental agent team reach out just cold call and try to extract information manually.
So, you know, that's, that's where I say it's not a total reliance on apartment IQ.
We kind of fill in the blanks as needed as well.
Sure, that makes sense.
Yeah.
And most folks I talk to like to see apartment IQ as a compliment to a couple of other tools.
So I'm always interested to just understand exactly how it's working for your team.
Um And then as far as main users, just you right now, is there anybody else you'd like to be in the platform when I take a look?
Um Of course, I can see that there are a couple other names besides yourself.
Um, but is there anybody else who should be logging in regularly or groups you'd like to see logging in that I can reach out to and, and get involved?
Yeah, I mean, at any given point, I'd say from our management team or the executive group, I'd probably be the only user from that team that goes in.
If anything, I'd have some subordinates or, you know, our, our interns accounting people that I would have access it sooner than our other, uh, executives.
But, like, I think right now there might be like a V Peterson as another user.
He was, uh, um, he was working with me over the summer.
I had him doing some stuff with it.

Um Well, I mean, we have a property accountant that I might look to have run some reports in it too so I can lead that charge as needed as I need to have people maybe do some digging or we'll put something together for me.
I can just put them in contact with you as needed.
Fair enough.
That sounds great.

And if you know, if we have uh perhaps an intern position, which is really common, uh a lot of turnover there, we can just create a general user name that can be shared as folks come in and out or as folks need.
So I'll keep that, that, that
makes sense.
Um Yeah, generic email.
If you could set something like that up and give me the credentials, I can share it with whoever whenever needed.
Then.
Terrific.
Terrific.
All right.
So we'll plan for that.
Um And then happy to pop in here and highlight a couple of things I noticed, uh, I gave the account a brief once over just to, you know, to make sure that I knew what's happening here.
I was noticing a couple of NAS coming through on some of these, of, of comps across a few markets.
What that typically tells me is that we've not run our thresholds in a little while for these comps.
Um, perhaps some are outdated, perhaps some are no longer relevant to you.
And if they are, we have made a lot of changes to our scraper since you guys joined.
I think I saw the contract start date in 2022.
Um So I'd love to have the opportunity to redo these.
Um So what, what that involves just me asking my team to rerun the thresholds here.
We can either include some properties that apartment IQ would recommend we call that a dynamic comp set or we can leave it to just the properties that you have here.
If you feel that this is most representative of your comp set groups, um What a lot of my customers and I will do, we'll do a little bit of a project.
They send me their eight or 12 Best Fit comps and ask me to add maybe two or three that the system thinks fit as well.
So that can be commonplace.
But you let me know what you think about that, Lee, how does that, how does that sound either of those
options?
I think that makes sense.
Um Let me just see what timeline we're kind of on right now when I would look to have this.
But
yeah, I know this can be a busy time.
You know, the first phase of Q four is over, but we've got the holidays coming up.
What's your, uh, leasing season?
Like
we are very seasonal.
We go from April until August at this point.
You know, we're above 98% occupied.
That's the, is always to be pretty much wrapped up by Labor Day.
Uh This year, we were pretty aggressive in our rent increases.
So we saw a little bit of carry over past our norm.
But, um, you know, right now, we've got maybe a six month lull until we pick back up again.
And it looks like the last time I did a run through and adjusted the market rents was in August.
So, I mean, I'd probably be looking in late November, early December to really dig into this and then have new pricing effective 1124.

So I would say if we do what you mentioned in a new scrub and getting a new uh set of comps, you know, I can take that project on with you in the next couple of weeks and then have it run for the end of November and I can start working with it in early December.
So, you know, roughly a month from now, I'd like to see that, uh, redone and ready to go.
Absolutely.
Ok.
I've made a note of that myself.
Definitely a doable, doable task in, in four weeks.
Absolutely.
So that by 11 you're ready and you've got everything you need to know to set those rents for 2024.
Um So made that note there.
Another thing I wanted to bring to your attention kind of as part of the, uh, you know, changes that we've brought in.
We've got this market survey tab here.
Um Super preliminary uh it is in beta right now, but I wanted to turn it on just to give you an idea of what this is gonna look like.

Uh What we're finding perhaps for folks who have a large uh community manager user base, is that something like this is a lot more familiar for them, a lot easier to use compared to the existing reports that exist in our property tab and unit type tab.
So just wanted to give you an idea of the kind of changes that are coming.
This is not for general release quite yet.
Uh But this will be a part of apartment IQ.
Uh I wanna say end of Q four, if not 1120 to 24.
OK.
And then other things that have changed as well.
We brought in concessions and concession details.

We did not have an effective way of capturing them before now, we're capturing upwards of 92% of all concessions on the market because we can do that because we can pull the number, we can then deliver an average n er, and we can do that on a per unit basis as well.
So I'm not sure when I did that preliminary check, I didn't see a whole lot of concessions active in your market.
I'm not sure.
Are they relevant in your markets right now or not quite,

um, definitely relevant, but I, I would think they would be hard to, to pull at some of these places that might be, you know, a little bit more a la carte or not broadcasting them and then using it as part of a sales pitch, I mean, we're even guilty of that but where we will kind of turn that on and off without marketing it widespread.
Um, so yeah, I mean, it's great info if, if it's uh popping up, but I wouldn't almost, I wouldn't trust the lack of it to mean that there's none of it out there right now,
right.
So in your market, you'd say it's definitely still necessary.
I mean, even if, you know, I, obviously we have N A here on, on average rents because we're not pulling from Clifton or Fox Brook.
Um, but regardless, there's still a manual component that's necessary in your markets no matter
what I think so.
Yeah.
Ok.
That's interesting to know.
And, and again, I've been in a really exciting position getting to know a lot of our accounts over the last, uh, I guess, over the summer, um, and, and bringing that feedback to product.
So, if you wouldn't mind, I feel like you have a really interesting perspective because your market might be a little, I wanna say unique.
Um, I'd love to put your name on a short list of folks that my product team might reach out to, for some market research and kind of help us inform the product if that's all right.
Yeah, not a
problem.
Very cool.
All right, we've talked concessions.
We've talked to n er uh other things that I'd love to kind of highlight for you.

Our road map, a road map going into Q four, we've delivered concessions in N er we've talked about, I just gave you that little peek at the market survey tab something that will also be coming will be kind of a new fees and deposits experience as well as an occupancy.
We've kind of reconfigured our algorithms a little bit to better capture some of this occupancy information uh without necessarily diving into private data.
So again, our whole ethos here is public information.
I'm sure you've seen some of the lawsuits about that, that private information over the summer.
We're very much trying to steer clear of that.
It's created some nuances about talking about how we deliver some of this information.
It's created some setbacks in the past, right?
Because we're, we're working with what is published.
Um But we are finding a way to get closer and closer to a true occupancy.
Something I can't necessarily show you because it's not a feature, I just switch on and off.
The product is our daily data unit sync.
You had mentioned that you guys use a Google sheet to maneuver a lot of this data.
I understand that as the data is today, it's not as complete as we might like it.

But I think this could be an interesting thing to explore once we do our reconfiguration and see if more data is coming in because we can then pump that right into your spreadsheet on a daily or weekly basis depending on what works best for you and your models.
So something I'll keep in the back of my mind as we get through that project
and then I have these of course standard uh explanations about n er about our concessions.
Um The big news as, as part of us kind of coming together here.
You guys are on an older contract.
So there will be an effort from my billing team to see if we can get you guys into a more modern contract.
Um Don't be afraid of this pricing.
This is the floor pricing that every new customer at apartment IQ is going to get.
This is the absolute ceiling as an existing partner, we are going to work with you to do something here.

Um But you'll notice what we're trying to do is move towards a, a premium kind of tier subscription model, including concessions and er, that apartment 2.0 we're talking about the market survey there, um fees and deposits, uh and then the enterprise level stuff.
Uh, a lot of this can be mixed and matched, but we are doing a big effort to standardize
as far as our existing customers.
If none of that is interesting to you, I don't care about the daily data sync.
I don't care about concessions or n er I don't have, you know, P MS and community managers coming in.
So the market survey is not actually that helpful to me.
We can kind of leave everything as is and we're gonna be looking at a number very close to this basic tier here.
That'll still include your unit level reporting.
It'll include that PDF that we're about to talk about a little bit more um and the updates that are coming to it.
So just wanna kind of put this and float this your way.
Uh It's not a conversation we have to have today tomorrow next week.
Uh But I do know probably by the end of this quarter, if not 11, my billing team will be looking to me to say, hey, what's up update Peterson up to?
Can we get them on a on an updated contract?
So sure, do you know often?
So when the contract's up
Yeah.
So you guys are right now on a month to month contract.
Um, because I believe your contract converted month to month in March of this year.
Let me see.
Uh, we had your apartment IQ for Amherst Manor, Deer Lakes, Liberty Square, London Town, Old Town and Peppertree.
Uh, those all went to month to month.
Got it.
All right.
And so I believe you are on about 95 $85 a property.
Um, so not too much will change if you just want to keep everything as is, uh, but if anything about those concessions or, or market, uh, surveys are interesting.
We can talk about that and see if we can get you something that feels like a middle ground between a 100% increase and, and what you'll end at that.
All right.
Now, let's talk about the PDF.
Let's see.
Let's download a PDF.
Whoops, wrong button.
So, have you explored the automatic delivery of this, by the way?
Not yet.
I actually, the owner of our company may have because he got the first round of the PDF.
Uh, summaries forwarded it to me and, you know, I, I had this call lined up so I have not really don't, uh, I haven't dove it, uh, dived in, dive into it quite yet.
Um,
yeah.
Yeah.
All right.
Well, I think, um, if the automatic reporting sounds nice, that's easy enough to configure
probably opt into that for sure.
But, uh, the way they're all structured, we've got the stock photo.
We have the date that this was prepared by.
And of course, that's really important because we know apartment IQ was a day behind.
Right.
We're scraping every night from about nine pm, 10 p.m. Eastern all the way till one o'clock in the morning.
So that when you walk in the next day at 9 a.m. you've got yesterday's data
first tab quick geographic idea.
Where are we in the market?
Where are we in the world?
And then five key performance indicators to serve as benchmarks so that anybody reading this data knows where the market is on average.
Then we have the property tab information first
followed by the unit type analysis for each of the floor plans that you had availability at last night.
Some feedback we've heard on this actually is that we'd prefer to be able to choose which floor plans come through here.
So let's say I have four bedrooms at my flex, but they're all filled up.
I'd like to still see what the market's doing and send that to my team.
So that's the kind of feedback we've been hearing and the kind of changes we'll have.
Um additionally, the ability to reconfigure what metrics are showing up in these columns,

the changes that are happening will have trend lines, arrows going up or down to let you know where the movement has been here, particularly when we're talking about things like days on market average rents.
Are, is this going up or down?
And how are we comparing to last week or the last month that we looked at this?
Any questions you have about how you might be using this or kind of best practices here even?
No, no.
I think this part is pretty straightforward.
Cool.
Is there anything that I've missed or haven't talked about anything you'd like to, you'd like to bring up or chat about?
Yeah, just a couple points.
Um And even if they're takeaways for you to, you know, bring to your team, the
the hardest piece of our portfolio that, you know, we all struggle with.
Uh is the fact that we have 14 apartment communities that we have these summaries for, but we have 500 units that are for single family homes, duplexes and condoms.
They're not a, a traditional property that has multiple buildings and you know, 100 to 200 units.
It is uh what we call the scattered portfolio.
So all of these are unique duplexes or single family homes that are very difficult to establish comps for and to date.
I know we don't really have anything built in to apartment IQ to give us any, any real time information on the competition out there.
There's just not a lot of competition.
Obviously, you know, you have smaller landlords who might have a handful of uh duplexes but, you know, they're not on yard, they're not really even advertising online.
Many, in many cases, they might have a sign in front of their, uh, unit or
Craigslist, Facebook Marketplace Post.
Right.
Yeah,
exactly.
So, that's a really hard market for us.
Um, takes a lot of manual, you know, I'm working in the MLS and doing different things to try to find some comps there.
So that's still, you know, the white whale for us.

And then, you know, I don't know if your, your clients have mentioned, especially those that integrate with yardi, which we are heavily uh associated to yardi the revenue IQ feature that they have, which is like a dynamic uh price model with day to day suggestions.

I mean, it's, it's not this similar from what apartment IQ is, but it feels like it would be uh more of a unit by unit looking at our own inventory of what we have available pricing recommendations to the unit, not so much the unit type or the property within the competition.
So uh I don't know what you can maybe share on that front, but those were the two notes I wanted to bring up.

No, I definitely, I absolutely hear your hear your frustration on the the smaller, the smaller properties, the the single families, the duplexes I hear that a lot uh with so unfortunately, nothing on our immediate for uh road map to, to kind of ease that pain point um but it is something I'm gonna bring up again.
I can't even actually think of another service in the industry that would do something similar to what we're doing for that, for that market.
So I think right now that is indeed a bit of a white whale um to try and find now the second point you bring up the recommendations.
Uh to be, to be quite honest, I was actually on a call this morning.
It sounds like we're gonna be sunsetting our recommendations feature.

It sounds like as of November 1, uh no new customers are going to be brought in with the recommendations to have even turned on and we'll be looking to probably turn it off for everyone uh towards the back of the uh Q form that said we were all kind of like, well, what the heck, where is it going?
Um There is an effort to rebuild this in such a way, that's way more intelligent and way more dynamic and that will be per unit compared to your true comps.
So that sounds like a point where we could definitely use your perspective as well as we start talking about flushing that out more because the idea is for it to return.

Um They wanted to say first half of next year um to return in a much more comprehensive way because right now, it really is just kind of looking at the averages in the market and, and telling you where you are based on that and you know, that that's not terribly valuable for folks who are so data driven.

I mean, if you're looking at the kinds of data you're looking at in this portfolio of units and then you just get surfaced in average, that's probably not fitting into your, you know, your analysis criteria, right?
A bit high level.

Um So I will say we are coming back strong with the recommendations feature and, and I, what I can say is I'd love to have your feedback on that when it happens, but nothing in the short term, unfortunately,
but thank you so much for sharing that because one of my favorite things to do in these calls is kind of ask, hey, if you had a magic wand, right?
What are you gonna change about apartment IQ tomorrow?
So that this works better for you?
Um Sounds like the ability to track that scattered portfolio would be one and having a, a more comprehensive or, or, or, or intelligent recommendations tool.
Yeah.
And you know, for us, a lot of uh the inferences that we make, you know, we, we may set these market rents with the best intentions and information available to us.
But in real time, we're having to react to, you know, the market conditions.

So whether it's a high unit count, uh particular property, having more inventory than we would like or length of time spent on inventory are two main factors that will really initiate us to maybe drive down uh an individual units price um against what maybe the market rent had that's been established to be or what the averages are showing.

So that dynamic function like that's a manual effort on my part where I'm running our inventory looking at how long it's been there and, you know, trying to um make some of these decisions in real time.
So, uh, Apartment Iqiw, I would wanna say at least that I'm aware of there wouldn't be, uh, information at the fingertips that say things like length of time a unit's been on inventory.
Um, you know, that's an important piece to, to our puzzle at least.
Right,
right.
Length of time.
Yeah, because being on because I mean, is days on market the same, that same metric or, or not quite.
Yeah,
I mean, the only layer that we might go a little further with is, uh, days vacant, um, days vacant.
Yeah.
Ok.
That's good to know.
I'm
looking at it here.
So you're right days on market.
If I'm looking at deer lakes, that's definitely there.
Um But like that would be a trigger to me with all these deer lakes units that we have available.
Um,
probably to what we call lost leaders, we drive down those rents a couple of units.
Um So, you know, for me, the recommend recommended rent is not so valuable.

I, I honestly kind of disregard that as far as apartment IQ goes, I rely on apartment IQ more for the comp, uh, sets that they give on the average for the square square footage or, you know, a particular two bedroom set.
But, um, sure, I guess to be honest, I'm not, uh, disappointed to hear that that feature is leaving necessarily, but I can tell you, we are, we are pretty intrigued by the Yardie product.
Um, it's just kind of our philosophy here to do as many Yardie Boltons.
That makes sense for us.
Um I don't know, I mean, I know what that cost is.
It's, it's probably pretty close to what we're paying for apartment IQ and rentable as a marketing source.
And, you know, we're, we're rolling out a couple of different things right now.
So it's not on the immediate horizon, but I would assume into 2024 we'll probably be having these conversations about, you know, if we go with this yardi product, do we want to continue apartment IQ?
Do we see benefits with both?
Um But yeah, that's probably two or three months out from really being uh at the forefront for us,
right?
Ok.
That makes sense.
And I think, uh I think that kind of coincides even with the, the timeline I mentioned without even talking about our contract situation.

I'd love to maybe have another call perhaps with our director of product and understand what it is.
That yard is doing really well for you.

Because at the end of the day, I want it to be a fit.

sure.
